activities_projects.title: Food Chain and Food Web Model/Chart
activities_projects.objective: Understand energy flow and trophic levels
steps: Choose an ecosystem (e.g., pond, forest, grassland)
Draw producers, primary/secondary/tertiary consumers, decomposers
Show arrows for energy flow (10% rule)
Compare linear food chain vs complex food web
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains interdependence and energy transfer in ecosystems
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of trophic levels, arrows, and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Biodegradable vs Non-Biodegradable Waste Survey
steps: Collect 20 household/school waste items
Categorize as biodegradable/non-biodegradable
Note decomposition time estimates
Discuss impact on environment
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes waste types and need for segregation
activities_projects.evaluation: Classification accuracy and discussion points (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ozone Layer Depletion Poster
activities_projects.objective: Illustrate causes, effects, and protection of ozone
steps: Draw Earth's atmosphere layers with ozone shield
Show CFC release, ozone hole formation over Antarctica
List effects (UV radiation increase, skin cancer, crop damage)
Mention Montreal Protocol
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands ozone's role and human impact
activities_projects.evaluation: Diagram clarity, facts accuracy, creativity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ecosystem Components Observation
steps: Observe a small local ecosystem (garden, park, pond)
List 10 biotic (plants, animals) and 10 abiotic (soil, water, light) factors
Note interactions (e.g., plants providing shade, insects pollinating)
Present findings
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness of lists and interactions noted (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Biological Magnification Demonstration/Model
activities_projects.objective: Show accumulation of toxins in food chain
materials: Chart paper or jars with colored beads/beans
steps: Model food chain: producers → herbivores → carnivores
Use beads to represent pesticide (increase at each level)
Show magnification in top consumers
Explain DDT example and human impact
activities_projects.evaluation: Model clarity and scientific explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Waste Management Plan for School/Home
activities_projects.objective: Promote 5R principles (Reduce, Reuse, Recycle, Refuse, Recover)
steps: Survey waste generated in school/home for a week
Propose practical ways to reduce waste
Suggest recycling methods and composting
Make awareness poster
activities_projects.evaluation: Practicality of plan and poster quality (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Energy Flow in Ecosystem Diagram
steps: Draw pyramid of energy/numbers/biomass
Show energy loss as heat at each trophic level
Calculate example: 1000 J at producers → 100 J herbivores → 10 J carnivores
Explain why food chains are short

activities_projects.title: Chapter Quiz on Environment Concepts
steps: Prepare 15-20 questions (MCQs, short answers) on ecosystem, ozone, waste, food chains
Conduct group quiz
Discuss answers with explanations

activities_projects.title: Composting Experiment
steps: Set up compost pile/layer in pot
Observe over 2-3 weeks (turn occasionally)
Record changes (temperature, smell, decomposition)
Compare with non-biodegradable item left aside

activities_projects.title: Innovative Idea: Eco-Friendly Solution
activities_projects.objective: Apply knowledge to solve environmental problem
steps: Identify local issue (plastic waste, pollution)
Sketch innovative solution (e.g., reusable bag design, waste-to-energy idea)
Explain how it reduces impact on environment
Present to class
